title: Zillow launches Zillow Preview to bring pre-market home listings into the open
lead: Zillow today introduced Zillow Preview, a new listing category that allows sellers to test market interest before officially listing their home. Properties in Preview status are visible to shoppers but excluded from third-party syndication. Participating brokerages can opt-in sellers starting April 1.

title: Zillow Preview gains rapid momentum as dozens of new brokerages sign on
lead: Zillow announced that more than 60 brokerage firms have joined its Zillow Preview program since the March launch. The company reported that Preview listings have generated 2.3x more saves per view than standard listings in participating markets. Zillow's chief industry development officer called the adoption 'faster than any product launch in the last five years'.

title: CoStar Group Launches Transformative AI Experience on Homes.com
lead: CoStar Group unveiled a redesigned Homes.com powered by generative AI that lets users search for properties using natural language descriptions. The system, built on proprietary CoStar data, can answer questions about neighborhoods, schools, and commute times directly from the search interface. CoStar CEO Andy Florance said the launch marks 'the end of filter-based search as the primary discovery mechanism'.

title: Zillow Goes AI Mode With New Home Search Assistant
lead: Zillow launched an AI-powered search assistant that allows homebuyers to describe what they are looking for in natural language. The assistant can parse requests such as 'find me a 3-bedroom home with a yard near good schools under $600K' and surface relevant listings without manual filter adjustments. The feature is currently available to 10% of users in a limited beta rollout.

title: Product and Services Roundup: ChatGPT Additions for Rightmove, Idealista and HepsiEmlak
lead: Three major real estate portals — Rightmove (UK), Idealista (Spain/Italy), and HepsiEmlak (Turkey) — rolled out ChatGPT-powered chatbots on their platforms within a two-week window. Each implementation allows buyers to ask property questions in natural language, with answers drawn from listing data and neighborhood guides. Rightmove says early data shows a 12% increase in time-on-site for users who engage with the chatbot.

title: Rightmove Sued for £1.5 Billion by UK Estate Agent Coalition
lead: A coalition of UK estate agencies has filed a £1.5 billion class-action lawsuit against Rightmove, alleging the portal abused its market-dominant position to impose excessive subscription fees. The claimants represent approximately 4,000 agencies and argue that Rightmove's pricing has increased by 50% over five years with no commensurate increase in leads or value delivered. The case is being handled by competition law specialist Hausfeld.

title: Scout24 acquires Spanish online real estate platforms Fotocasa and Habitaclia
lead: Scout24, the German digital marketplace group, has agreed to acquire Fotocasa and Habitaclia from Adevinta for €620 million. The two platforms together represent Spain's number two and number three real estate portals by traffic, with a combined audience of 12 million monthly visitors. The deal is expected to close in Q3 2026 pending EU regulatory approval.

title: CoStar eliminates Domain's Australian strategy, product and tech teams
lead: CoStar Group has dissolved the strategy, product, and technology divisions at Domain, the Australian real estate portal it acquired in late 2025. The functions will be absorbed into CoStar's global team based in the US. Approximately 120 staff in Sydney and Melbourne are affected by the restructuring, which CoStar says will complete by end of Q2.

title: Zumper CEO to Transition to Boardroom, CRO Steps Up
lead: Zumper founder and CEO Anthemos Georgiades announced he will move to the company's board of directors after ten years leading the rental marketplace. Chief Revenue Officer Matt Curtis has been appointed President and will assume day-to-day operating responsibility effective July 1. The company said the transition is part of a planned evolution toward profitability.

title: Czech Regulator Forces Sreality Pricing Reset After Abuse of Dominance Finding
lead: The Czech Office for the Protection of Competition ruled that Sreality, the dominant Czech real estate portal owned by Seznam, must reduce subscription prices for real estate agencies by 35% within 90 days. The regulator found that Sreality leveraged its market position to charge supra-competitive rates, with agency fees rising 80% since 2018. Seznam avoided direct fines by agreeing to the pricing reset voluntarily.

title: Home search is going AI-first with Realtor.com as attention gets harder to earn
lead: Realtor.com has overhauled its core search experience, making AI-driven intent parsing the primary discovery mechanism. Users no longer begin with a filter panel; instead, a conversational interface interprets buyer preferences and surfaces matching listings dynamically. The platform has simultaneously launched a native app integration with ChatGPT, enabling property search through dialogue across all devices.

title: Opendoor acquires Doma's closing, escrow and title technology stack
lead: Opendoor has completed the acquisition of the technology assets of Doma, a public proptech company, including its AI-driven title insurance platform and escrow automation suite. The acquisition is valued at approximately $150 million and gives Opendoor end-to-end transaction capability. Opendoor says the tools will be integrated into its Sell+Close product and made available to third-party agents within 12 months.

title: Avito Adtech expands self-serve analytics dashboard for advertising clients
lead: Avito has launched an expanded analytics dashboard for its advertising clients, giving sellers real-time access to impression data, audience demographics, and conversion metrics. The update includes a self-serve campaign management interface that allows businesses to adjust bids and targeting without account manager involvement. Avito says the rollout is part of a broader platform-wide move toward performance marketing transparency.

title: French Portal Rivalry Intensifies as Bien'ici, SeLoger, and Leboncoin Trade Claims
lead: France's leading real estate portals are engaged in a public dispute over traffic rankings and lead quality. SeLoger (part of Aviv Group) published data claiming a 15% lead quality advantage over Bien'ici, while Leboncoin accused both of inflating traffic figures. The rivalry has intensified since Bien'ici relaunched with a redesigned product and new pricing structure in January 2026.

title: How AI is transforming the rental application process
lead: Several proptech startups and large rental platforms are deploying AI tools to automate the rental application workflow, including income verification, background screening, and landlord-applicant matching. Companies like Funnel Leasing and Avail report 40-60% reductions in time-to-lease. RentSpree has introduced a 'smart queue' feature that ranks applicants by likelihood of successful tenancy and generates a recommendation to the landlord.

title: Homes.com Expands Pre-Market Listings Program Nationwide
lead: Homes.com has extended its pre-market listing feature to all 50 US states after a six-month pilot in 12 markets. The program allows sellers to list a property as 'coming soon' and receive buyer inquiries before the home is formally placed on the MLS. Participating agents can see and respond to buyer interest up to 30 days before the official listing date, with buyer contacts counted as exclusive leads for the listing agent.

title: Lofty AI launches end-to-end real estate CRM with full pipeline automation
lead: Lofty (formerly Chime) has released a fully redesigned CRM platform that uses AI agents to automate follow-ups, schedule viewings, and draft offer letters on behalf of real estate agents. The system integrates with major MLS feeds and can autonomously manage a pipeline of up to 200 leads with minimal manual input from the agent. Pricing starts at $500/month per agent seat with AI automation features unlocked.

title: CoStar Accuses Zillow of Ongoing IP Infringement in Expanded Amended Complaint
lead: CoStar Group has filed an amended lawsuit against Zillow, expanding its intellectual property infringement claims to include 9,000 additional property photographs and asserting that Zillow continues to use CoStar imagery without authorisation. The suit now also covers data allegedly copied from LoopNet, CoStar's commercial real estate platform. Legal observers say the expansion signals a strategy of exhausting Zillow through protracted litigation.

title: NWMLS files federal lawsuit against Compass over pocket listing practices
lead: Northwest Multiple Listing Service has filed a federal lawsuit against Compass, alleging the brokerage violated MLS participation rules by systematically marketing properties exclusively to its own agents before publicly listing them. NWMLS argues the practice harms sellers by reducing competitive buyer exposure and allowing Compass to capture both sides of transactions more frequently. The suit seeks injunctive relief and damages.

title: Compass touts record revenue as private listing strategy drives growth
lead: Compass reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.45 billion, up 18% year-over-year, citing its exclusive private listing network as the primary growth driver. The company said private listings now account for 12% of total transaction volume, with average days-on-market 40% lower than comparable MLS listings. Management called private listings 'a structurally durable advantage' on the earnings call.

title: New data shows AI search adoption by homebuyers climbing fast
lead: A survey of 2,400 US homebuyers by market research firm Bright MLS found that 38% used an AI-assisted search tool at some point in their most recent home purchase journey, up from 11% in 2024. Among buyers under 35, adoption reached 61%. Respondents rated natural language search as significantly less frustrating than filter-based alternatives, with 72% saying it 'better captured what I was looking for'.

title: Dubizzle Group: From classifieds to full property transaction platform in MENA
lead: Dubizzle Group, the UAE-based classifieds and real estate portal operator, has launched a full-service property transaction product in Dubai, allowing buyers and sellers to complete legal documentation, ownership transfer, and mortgage applications through the platform. The company processed over 200 complete end-to-end transactions in its pilot phase with a reported NPS of 68. Expansion to Abu Dhabi is planned for Q3.

title: Property Finder raises $400M, bets on product depth not geographic expansion
lead: Property Finder, the leading real estate portal in the UAE and broader MENA region, has closed a $400 million growth equity round led by KKR. CEO Michael Lahyani said the company will invest the capital in verified listings quality infrastructure, AI valuation tools, and mortgage pre-approval integrations with local banks rather than launching in new geographies. The company declined to disclose its current valuation.

title: Bazzile and Resolve integrate to offer agents a bundled mortgage-referral product
lead: French proptech Bazzile has announced a commercial integration with mortgage broker Resolve, enabling agents on the Bazzile network to offer pre-qualified mortgage options directly from the listing page. Agents who join Bazzile's network receive exclusive access to Resolve's qualified buyer pipeline in exchange for a shared commission on completed mortgage originations. The model is designed to reduce agent acquisition costs while increasing quality leads.

title: CoStar Group Completes Acquisition of Visual Lease for $272 Million
lead: CoStar Group has completed its $272 million acquisition of Visual Lease, a lease accounting and administration software company used by corporate real estate teams. The acquisition extends CoStar's data platform from market intelligence into enterprise lease management, which the company estimates is a $2 billion addressable market. Visual Lease serves over 1,500 corporate clients managing more than 500,000 leases.

title: Realtor.com launches Verified Seller badge to reduce buyer drop-off at due diligence
lead: Realtor.com has introduced a 'Verified Seller' program that displays a trust badge on listings where the selling agent has confirmed key property details including condition disclosures, title history, and any known defects. The badge is available exclusively to Premier Agent subscribers and is backed by a liability indemnification clause. Realtor.com's product team says early A/B tests showed a 19% reduction in buyer abandon rate at the offer stage.

title: Redfin and Opendoor launch joint platform letting sellers compare instant offer vs. traditional listing
lead: Redfin and Opendoor have announced a data-sharing and referral partnership that presents sellers with both a traditional listing recommendation and an Opendoor cash offer side-by-side in a single Redfin interface. The companies will share lead data and a revenue percentage from completed transactions through either path. The integration goes live across 25 US markets in May 2026.

title: Rightmove introduces new three-tier subscription model for estate agencies
lead: Rightmove has unveiled a redesigned subscription structure for estate agents that replaces the single monthly fee with three tiers: Essential, Professional, and Enterprise. The Enterprise tier, priced at £2,400 per month per branch, includes priority listing placement, AI-powered automated market appraisal, and an enhanced analytics dashboard. Rightmove says the change is designed to 'better align pricing with value delivered' to high-volume clients.

title: REA Group launches guaranteed floor-price program for Australian home sellers
lead: REA Group's realestate.com.au has introduced a program allowing sellers to lock in a minimum sale price guaranteed by a network of institutional buyers. If the property fails to sell above the guaranteed price within 60 days of listing, an institutional buyer purchases it at the floor price, with REA earning a transaction fee. The program launches in Melbourne and Sydney, with Brisbane to follow in Q3.

title: Homes.com reaches 30M monthly visitors as US portal traffic sees biggest shift in a decade
lead: New Similarweb data shows Homes.com surpassed 30 million monthly unique visitors in March 2026, up from 8 million a year ago, following heavy advertising spend and its AI search relaunch. Zillow remains the market leader with 220 million monthly visits, while Realtor.com has declined to 68 million from 88 million twelve months prior. Industry analysts say the shift represents the fastest competitive traffic realignment in US residential real estate since Zillow's own launch.

title: Agent Pens Open Letter to Rightmove After 12% Price Hike
lead: A UK estate agent operating 15 branches published an open letter condemning Rightmove's decision to raise subscription fees by 12% in January 2026, the latest in a series of annual increases. The letter, circulated among an estimated 2,000 agents via industry forums, argues that Rightmove's market dominance allows it to extract fees with no competitive constraint. Industry observers note it signals a growing coalition of agent discontent that could escalate into formal action.

title: Yandex Q4 Revenue Up 28% as Real Estate Sits Within AI Engine
lead: Yandex reported Q4 2025 revenue growth of 28% year-over-year, with its real estate vertical playing a central role inside its YandexGPT AI search engine. Property listings are now surfaced through conversational AI responses to user queries, generating a 31% increase in real estate session depth compared to filter-based search. The company described the integration model — real estate as a native vertical inside a generalist AI engine — as its primary competitive moat against specialist property portals.

title: Homes.com moves toward filterless search with AI assistant
lead: CoStar Group's Homes.com has replaced its traditional filter panel with a conversational AI assistant as the default search interface. Users can now describe preferences in natural language — such as 'something near downtown with a yard and space for a home office' — and receive ranked listing recommendations without setting price, bedroom, or bathroom filters. Inman reports Homes.com is the first US portal to make AI-powered natural language search the default rather than an optional add-on.

title: People Roundup: Boneo CEO Departs, Zillow Promotes Three
lead: OnlineMarketplaces.com's monthly executive movement roundup covers two notable leadership changes in the global portal sector. Boneo, the emerging Scandinavian portal challenger, saw its founding CEO depart after 18 months following reported differences with the board over growth strategy. At Zillow, three senior leaders — the Chief Operating Officer, Head of Product Marketing, and VP of Audience Growth — received promotions from within, signalling organisational continuity at the US market leader as it executes its AI search pivot.

title: Investment and Funding Roundup: Zoopla Closes Acquisition, Grupo OLX Enters Joint Venture
lead: OnlineMarketplaces.com reports two M&A transactions from February 2026. Zoopla, the UK's second-largest property portal, has closed its acquisition of a residential data analytics firm, giving it proprietary pricing signal tools for agent and seller clients. Grupo OLX, the global classifieds group, has entered a joint venture with a Southeast Asian regional partner to accelerate local market expansion. The deals add to an active Q1 in portal-sector M&A following a two-year slowdown.

title: Compass partners with Rocket-Redfin to display coming-soon listings
lead: Compass struck a data-sharing agreement with the Rocket-Redfin platform to display Compass Private Exclusive coming-soon listings on Redfin's portal, which serves 50 million monthly visitors. The deal marks a significant strategic reversal: Redfin had previously been part of the public-listing coalition opposing Compass's pre-market model and aligned with Zillow's open-MLS policy. The partnership gives Compass a major distribution channel for its Private Exclusive inventory without requiring MLS submission.

title: Product and Services Roundup: ChatGPT Integrations for Redfin, REA Group, Leboncoin
lead: Three major global real estate portals integrated ChatGPT into their property search experiences in February 2026. Redfin (US, 30 million monthly visitors), REA Group's realestate.com.au (Australia's market leader), and Leboncoin (France, 25 million monthly) each launched ChatGPT-powered natural language search assistants within a two-week window. The wave follows the release of OpenAI's operator API in November 2025, which reduced portal integration costs by an estimated 80% and is expected to drive further adoption across global markets.

title: CoStar EBITDA Surges 83% as Domain to Fold Into Homes.com
lead: CoStar Group's Q4 2025 earnings showed EBITDA of $142 million, an 83% increase year-over-year, driven by Homes.com subscription growth and commercial data services revenue. Alongside the financial results, the company confirmed its most consequential post-acquisition decision: Domain, the Australian property portal acquired for $1.9 billion in Q3 2025, will not operate as a standalone brand. Domain will be fully integrated into the Homes.com global platform by Q3 2026, with its Sydney and Melbourne product teams already being centralised into CoStar's US operations.

title: RealScout scores a hat trick with massive AI-led update to platform
lead: RealScout, a property search platform used by over 4,000 brokerage offices in the US, has launched a simultaneous three-feature AI update. The package includes an AI buyer matching engine that proactively surfaces listings to buyers based on behavioural history, an automated home alert system using natural language preference modelling, and a real-time market analysis tool generating neighbourhood-level summaries on demand. The company said the entire update was built on the OpenAI API and shipped in six weeks.

title: Rightmove FY2025: Strong Results and Accelerated Innovation as Profits Hit Double Digit Growth
lead: Rightmove reported full-year 2025 revenue of £366 million, up 11% year-over-year, with operating profit margins reaching 71% — the highest in its 25-year history. CEO Johan Svanstrom credited accelerated product innovation, including AI-powered property valuation tools and a 27% increase in agent automation features, as key drivers. The company said it plans to reinvest £45 million in AI and data services in 2026, even as agent dissatisfaction with subscription pricing continues to mount.

title: Judge Denies Compass Bid to Block Zillow Listings Ban
lead: A federal judge in California denied Compass's motion for a preliminary injunction that would have blocked enforcement of Zillow's rule requiring any publicly marketed home to be submitted to the local MLS within one business day. The ruling allows Zillow to continue excluding listings that bypass MLS submission from its platform. Compass's Private Exclusive program — which routes listings to Compass agents only before public marketing — would violate Zillow's policy, effectively forcing sellers to choose between Compass exclusivity and Zillow exposure.

title: AI-Native Challenger Portals Gather Pace Across Global Markets
lead: A new cohort of AI-native property portals has emerged across global markets, built from scratch with conversational search as the core product rather than retrofitted onto existing listing databases. Key players include Dwelly (UK), Homebird (Ireland), and Nestful (Australia), all launched in the past six months and collectively attracting over $120 million in venture funding. Industry analysts cited in the piece say these challengers could capture 5-8% of listing search traffic within three years if incumbent portals fail to replicate the user experience quality.

title: eXp Realty launches AI tools for agent productivity
lead: eXp Realty has released a suite of AI-powered productivity tools to its network of 88,000 agents globally, embedded in the company's existing brokerage platform at no additional charge. The tools include an automated listing description generator, a market analysis dashboard with natural language query capability, and a client communication drafting assistant that adapts tone to buyer profile. The company reports the tools reduce per-agent administrative time by an average of 4.5 hours per week in internal testing.

title: CoStar Group Appoints Nana Banerjee to Board of Directors
lead: CoStar Group has appointed Nana Banerjee, former CEO of cybersecurity firm SecureWorks and a veteran of enterprise cloud infrastructure, to its board of directors effective March 15, 2026. Banerjee will serve on the Audit Committee and the newly formed Technology and Data Security Committee. CoStar cited her expertise in data governance and cloud-scale operations as directly relevant to its growing real estate data platform and the regulatory scrutiny that accompanies its market position.

title: Class action accuses lender of unsolicited AI-generated cold calls
lead: A class action lawsuit filed in the Southern District of New York alleges that a major US mortgage lender deployed an AI voice system to make over 2 million unsolicited calls to prospective borrowers, with the system impersonating a live loan officer. The suit claims violations of the Telephone Consumer Protection Act and seeks damages of up to $1,500 per call. Legal observers say this is the first US class action specifically targeting AI-generated voice marketing in financial services, and could establish precedent for AI outreach practices across real estate and mortgage.
